message:
refactor: registry event sequence construction and test assertion hardening

Registry conformance test cleanup:
- Refactor conformance_event_ordering_stable_across_seeds to construct
  the canonical event sequence using a vec![] literal inside
  build_event_sequence(), eliminating the mutable Vec + push pattern
  for a more idiomatic and readable construction
- Move init_test() call after the inner function definition to satisfy
  item-before-use ordering
- Replace i as u64 cast with u64::from(i) in the stress test loop
  for explicit, lossless integer conversion (clippy pedantic)

Supervision test assertion hardening:
- Replace assert!(false, ...) with unreachable!(...) across 7 match
  arms in conformance tests: escalation_without_parent_region,
  evidence_ledger crossproduct (Stop/Restart/Escalate branches),
  restart_delay_matches_backoff (3 attempts), and
  spawn_required_vs_optional_child_failure
- These branches represent logically impossible states that would
  indicate a broken test invariant, making unreachable!() semantically
  more accurate than a conditional assertion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
